name: Oracle Corporation
status_note: Publicly traded (NYSE: ORCL); Oracle Corporation remains active as of 2026 and is one of the largest enterprise software companies globally. Headquarters relocated from Redwood City, CA to Austin, TX in 2020. Revenue in 1995 was approximately $3 billion.

name: Aberdeen Group
status_note: Aberdeen Group was acquired by Harte-Hanks in 2001; research brand continued as Aberdeen.com and later Aberdeen Group under various ownership structures.

name: Microsoft Corporation
status_note: Publicly traded (NASDAQ: MSFT); still active as of 2026. Referenced in context of MAPI standard and Windows 95 In-Box.

name: Lotus Development Corporation
status_note: Acquired by IBM in 1995 for $3.5 billion; Lotus Notes evolved into IBM Lotus Notes and subsequently HCL Notes after HCL Technologies acquired the product line from IBM in 2019.

name: Novell, Inc.
status_note: Novell was acquired by Attachmate in 2011; Attachmate itself acquired by Micro Focus in 2014; Micro Focus acquired by OpenText in 2023. Referenced in context of groupware/messaging market competition.

name: Sun Microsystems, Inc.
status_note: Acquired by Oracle Corporation in 2010 for approximately $7.4 billion. Referenced in context of planned Java integration with Oracle InterOffice.

name: Hewlett-Packard Company
status_note: Split into HP Inc. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise in 2015; both entities remain active as of 2026. Referenced as HP-9000 server platform supported by Oracle InterOffice.

name: IBM Corporation
status_note: IBM Corporation remains active as of 2026 (NYSE: IBM). Referenced as RS/6000 server platform supported by Oracle InterOffice.
